title: Silky Smooth French Duck Rillettes
description: A cultured and adventurous French dish from Normandy featuring khus khus and tadka, this keto-friendly rillette is a must-try for any food enthusiast. It has a unique blend of flavors and textures.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the duck legs with salt, black pepper, and tadka spices.
In a large Dutch oven, melt the ghee over medium heat and sear the duck legs until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven to cook for 2 hours, or until the duck is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the duck meat with two forks and stir in the khus khus.
Transfer the mixture to a food processor and process until smooth and creamy.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Spoon the rillettes into individual serving cups and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.
